    org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String>
    aspectRatio()
    The aspect ratio of a video is a value that describes the relative shape of a video based on its width and height.

    builder()
     
    Optional<AiResponseRecord>
    chapters()
    Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.
    Optional<OffsetDateTime>
    createdAt()
    Time the media was created, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).
    Optional<String>
    creatorId()
    The unique identifier of the user who created this media.

    The length of the media in seconds, with a maximum allowed duration of 12 hours per individual media.

    Indicates whether the media contains only audio (no video track).

    The maximum resolution specified by the user for the media.

    The quality tier applied to the media.

    You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key" : "value" pairs.

  • Constructor Details

    • Media

      public Media(@Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> thumbnail, @Nullable String id, @Nullable String workspaceId, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Map<String,String>> metadata, @Nullable MediaMediaQuality mediaQuality, @Nullable String creatorId, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> title, @Nullable MediaMaxResolution maxResolution, @Nullable MediaSourceResolution sourceResolution, @Nullable MediaStatus status, @Nullable MediaMp4Support mp4Support, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> sourceAccess, @Nullable List<PlaybackId> playbackIds, @Nullable List<MediaTrack> tracks, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<List<TracksSubtitles>> generatedSubtitles, @Nullable AiSummaryRecord summary, @Nullable AiResponseRecord chapters, @Nullable AiResponseRecord namedEntities, @Nullable AiResponseRecord moderation, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> isAudioOnly, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> subtitleAvailable, @Nullable String duration, @Nullable org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> aspectRatio, @Nullable OffsetDateTime createdAt, @Nullable OffsetDateTime updatedAt)

    • Media

      public Media()

  • Method Details

    • thumbnail

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> thumbnail()
      A video thumbnail is a still image that acts as the preview image for your video.

    • id

      public Optional<String> id()
      The unique identifier assigned to the media when created. The value must be a valid UUID.

    • workspaceId

      public Optional<String> workspaceId()
      A unique identifier is generated by FastPix for the workspace.

    • metadata

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Map<String,String>> metadata()
      You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key" : "value" pairs. Dynamic metadata allows you to define a key that allows any value pair. You can have maximum of 255 characters and upto 10 entries are allowed.

    • mediaQuality

      public Optional<MediaMediaQuality> mediaQuality()
      The quality tier applied to the media.

    • creatorId

      public Optional<String> creatorId()
      The unique identifier of the user who created this media.

    • title

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> title()
      Title of the media file.

    • maxResolution

      public Optional<MediaMaxResolution> maxResolution()
      The maximum resolution specified by the user for the media.

    • sourceResolution

      public Optional<MediaSourceResolution> sourceResolution()
      The actual resolution of the uploaded media. This represents the native quality of the source media.

    • status

      public Optional<MediaStatus> status()
      Determines the media’s status, which can be one of the possible values.

    • mp4Support

      public Optional<MediaMp4Support> mp4Support()
      Determines the type of MP4 support for the media. - **none**: Disables MP4 support. - **capped_4k**: Enables MP4 downloads with resolutions up to 4K. - **audioOnly**: Provides an MP4 stream containing only the audio. - **audioOnly,capped_4k**: Enables both MP4 video downloads (up to 4K) and an audio-only stream.

    • sourceAccess

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> sourceAccess()
      The sourceAccess parameter determines whether the original media file is accessible. Set to true to enable access or false to restrict it.

    • playbackIds

      public Optional<List<PlaybackId>> playbackIds()
      A collection of Playback ID objects utilized for crafting HLS playback URLs.

    • tracks

      public Optional<List<MediaTrack>> tracks()
      A media consists of different media tracks, like video, audio, and subtitle, all combined.

    • generatedSubtitles

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<List<TracksSubtitles>> generatedSubtitles()
      List of generated subtitle tracks associated with the media.

    • summary

      public Optional<AiSummaryRecord> summary()
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • chapters

      public Optional<AiResponseRecord> chapters()
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • namedEntities

      public Optional<AiResponseRecord> namedEntities()
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • moderation

      public Optional<AiResponseRecord> moderation()
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • isAudioOnly

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> isAudioOnly()
      Indicates whether the media contains only audio (no video track).

    • subtitleAvailable

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<Boolean> subtitleAvailable()
      Indicates whether subtitles are available for the media.

    • duration

      public Optional<String> duration()
      The length of the media in seconds, with a maximum allowed duration of 12 hours per individual media.

    • aspectRatio

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<String> aspectRatio()
      The aspect ratio of a video is a value that describes the relative shape of a video based on its width and height.

    • createdAt

      public Optional<OffsetDateTime> createdAt()
      Time the media was created, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • updatedAt

      public Optional<OffsetDateTime> updatedAt()
      Time the media was updated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • builder

      public static Media.Builder builder()

    • withThumbnail

      public Media withThumbnail(@Nullable String thumbnail)
      A video thumbnail is a still image that acts as the preview image for your video.

    • withId

      public Media withId(@Nullable String id)
      The unique identifier assigned to the media when created. The value must be a valid UUID.

    • withWorkspaceId

      public Media withWorkspaceId(@Nullable String workspaceId)
      A unique identifier is generated by FastPix for the workspace.

    • withMetadata

      public Media withMetadata(@Nullable Map<String,String> metadata)
      You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key" : "value" pairs. Dynamic metadata allows you to define a key that allows any value pair. You can have maximum of 255 characters and upto 10 entries are allowed.

    • withMediaQuality

      public Media withMediaQuality(@Nullable MediaMediaQuality mediaQuality)
      The quality tier applied to the media.

    • withCreatorId

      public Media withCreatorId(@Nullable String creatorId)
      The unique identifier of the user who created this media.

    • withTitle

      public Media withTitle(@Nullable String title)
      Title of the media file.

    • withMaxResolution

      public Media withMaxResolution(@Nullable MediaMaxResolution maxResolution)
      The maximum resolution specified by the user for the media.

    • withSourceResolution

      public Media withSourceResolution(@Nullable MediaSourceResolution sourceResolution)
      The actual resolution of the uploaded media. This represents the native quality of the source media.

    • withStatus

      public Media withStatus(@Nullable MediaStatus status)
      Determines the media’s status, which can be one of the possible values.

    • withMp4Support

      public Media withMp4Support(@Nullable MediaMp4Support mp4Support)
      Determines the type of MP4 support for the media. - **none**: Disables MP4 support. - **capped_4k**: Enables MP4 downloads with resolutions up to 4K. - **audioOnly**: Provides an MP4 stream containing only the audio. - **audioOnly,capped_4k**: Enables both MP4 video downloads (up to 4K) and an audio-only stream.

    • withSourceAccess

      public Media withSourceAccess(@Nullable Boolean sourceAccess)
      The sourceAccess parameter determines whether the original media file is accessible. Set to true to enable access or false to restrict it.

    • withPlaybackIds

      public Media withPlaybackIds(@Nullable List<PlaybackId> playbackIds)
      A collection of Playback ID objects utilized for crafting HLS playback URLs.

    • withTracks

      public Media withTracks(@Nullable List<MediaTrack> tracks)
      A media consists of different media tracks, like video, audio, and subtitle, all combined.

    • withGeneratedSubtitles

      public Media withGeneratedSubtitles(@Nullable List<TracksSubtitles> generatedSubtitles)
      List of generated subtitle tracks associated with the media.

    • withSummary

      public Media withSummary(@Nullable AiSummaryRecord summary)
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • withChapters

      public Media withChapters(@Nullable AiResponseRecord chapters)
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • withNamedEntities

      public Media withNamedEntities(@Nullable AiResponseRecord namedEntities)
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • withModeration

      public Media withModeration(@Nullable AiResponseRecord moderation)
      Represents an AI response record containing status and data for AI-generated features like summary, chapters, named entities, or moderation.

    • withIsAudioOnly

      public Media withIsAudioOnly(@Nullable Boolean isAudioOnly)
      Indicates whether the media contains only audio (no video track).

    • withSubtitleAvailable

      public Media withSubtitleAvailable(@Nullable Boolean subtitleAvailable)
      Indicates whether subtitles are available for the media.

    • withDuration

      public Media withDuration(@Nullable String duration)
      The length of the media in seconds, with a maximum allowed duration of 12 hours per individual media.

    • withAspectRatio

      public Media withAspectRatio(@Nullable String aspectRatio)
      The aspect ratio of a video is a value that describes the relative shape of a video based on its width and height.

    • withCreatedAt

      public Media withCreatedAt(@Nullable OffsetDateTime createdAt)
      Time the media was created, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • withUpdatedAt

      public Media withUpdatedAt(@Nullable OffsetDateTime updatedAt)
      Time the media was updated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).
